welcome.title: Welcome to the decision tree for the secure integration of AI systems
welcome.description: This decision tree helps financial institutions systematically review and implement the regulatory requirements of the EU AI Act and the DORA Regulation (Digital Operational Resilience Act).
welcome.expectItem1: Guided individual questions with a documented decision path
welcome.expectItem2: Coverage of the EU AI Act & DORA for financial institutions
welcome.expectItem3: Automatic alignment with relevant regulatory requirements
welcome.featuresItem1: Export of the path and outstanding requirements as a PDF
welcome.featuresItem2: Option to reset or restart the path
welcome.featuresItem3: Navigation to any step in the existing path
welcome.noteText: This application is intended for internal preliminary review and does not replace legal advice. For binding assessments, you should involve your legal department and external legal counsel.
welcome.durationSubline: (Quick check: ~15–30 min · Thorough review incl. evidence: 60+ min)
welcome.durationText: The duration depends on how many obligation packages are triggered and whether supporting evidence is directly available.
welcome.auditTrail: The path is fully logged during use.

creator.title: Who is conducting the assessment?
creator.description: Please enter the name or team completing the decision tree. This information is shown in the header and export.
creator.label: Creator (first name last name)

persistence.exportJsonTitle: Export assessment as a JSON file
persistence.importJsonTitle: Import assessment from a JSON file
persistence.managerDescription: Locally saved assessments can be loaded or deleted.
persistence.missingIdsWarning: This assessment contains nodes that were not found in the current model: {ids}

wizard.title: Decision tree for the secure integration of AI
wizard.resetVersionTitle: Resets the assessment ID (revision) to v1.0

leaf.prohibitedText: This scenario is generally prohibited under the EU AI Act. Stop use and trigger escalation.

summary.canContinue: You can continue with the integration.

consistency.bannerText: The wizard blocks the next step until the conflict is resolved.

a1NoButLaterAnswered.message: You answered “No” for “AI system?” (A1) but later answered additional EU AI Act steps. That is contradictory.
a1NoButLaterAnswered.recommendation1: Jump back to A1 and review the classification.
a1NoButLaterAnswered.recommendation2: If A1 is truly “No”, the flow should end at A0/ENDE and later answers are not meaningful.

highRiskDownstage.message: You already reached a high-risk path but later tried to switch to “not high-risk”. This is blocked in the wizard.
highRiskDownstage.recommendation1: Jump back to the marked step and correct the answer or add the justification.
highRiskDownstage.recommendation2: If unclear, classify conservatively (high-risk = yes) and apply the obligations.

highRiskConflict.message: A high-risk result has been reached, but the path also contains non-high-risk transparency/governance steps. That is contradictory.
highRiskConflict.noteNonHighRisk: Non-high-risk path step in the same flow
highRiskConflict.recommendation1: Jump back to the first non-high-risk step and review whether you classified something incorrectly earlier.
highRiskConflict.recommendation2: Document the classification clearly (use case, Annex III check, context).

prohibitedContinued.message: You reached a “prohibited practice” result but continued answering later steps. The wizard should stop here.
prohibitedContinued.recommendation1: Jump back to the prohibited result and stop/escalate as described.
prohibitedContinued.recommendation2: If your review concludes this is not a prohibited practice, return to the trigger step and adjust the answer.

doraRejectedButContinued.message: The DORA section was rejected (D0=No), but DORA questions were answered afterwards.
doraRejectedButContinued.recommendation1: Jump back to D0 and decide whether DORA is truly not applicable.
doraRejectedButContinued.recommendation2: If DORA is not applicable, remove/ignore downstream DORA steps and document the boundary.
